name: Saif ibn Malik
title: Lion of Basra
shortDescription: A sincere Shia from Basra who gathered at Mariya bint Munqidh's house and joined the Imam's caravan.
fullStory:
Sayf ibn Malik al-Abdi was a Shia from Basra. At that time, Basra was under the strict control of Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad, and leaving the city to join Imam Hussain (AS) was highly dangerous.

Sayf and his friends secretly gathered at the house of 'Mariya bint Munqidh' (a brave Shia woman in Basra) to plan their support for the Imam. Along with Yazid ibn Thubayt and a few others, he cleverly managed to sneak out of Basra and joined Imam Hussain (AS) on the way to Mecca.

On Ashura, Sayf defended the sanctuary of the Ahl al-Bayt with all his might. He fought valiantly and was martyred either in the first attack or during one-on-one combats.

His name is mentioned in the Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddasa, receiving the reward for his courage and risk-taking in Basra with martyrdom alongside the Master of Martyrs.
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Sayf ibn Malik...
